[eval_harness] Phase 2: Running evaluator
[eval_harness] Result: {'exprate': 0.0075, 'exprate_le1': 0.0392, 'exprate_le2': 0.0884, 'n_test': 1199}

trajectory.description: Observation: Starting from scratch. Analysis: Need to build a baseline Encoder-Decoder model for HMER. I will use DenseNet-121 as the encoder and a Transformer as the decoder. I will train for 2 epochs to verify the pipeline works and measure iteration time. Decision: Write dataset.py, model.py, train.py, and predict.py, then run training and submit eval.

eval_result.eval_log:
[eval_harness] Phase 1: Running predict script: /workspace/predict.py
  checkpoint: /workspace/checkpoints/best.pt
  data_path:  /data
  output:     /experiments/iter_002/eval_result_predictions.json
[eval_harness] Phase 2: Running evaluator
[eval_harness] Result: {'exprate': 0.0717, 'exprate_le1': 0.1435, 'exprate_le2': 0.226, 'n_test': 1199}

trajectory.description: Observation: The baseline model trained for 2 epochs achieved an exprate of 0.0075. The iteration took ~2.3 minutes. Analysis: The low score is expected for only 2 epochs. Additionally, the image size (128x512) with DenseNet-121 (stride 32) results in a very small feature map (4x16 = 64 tokens), which is insufficient to align with up to 150 text tokens. Decision: 1) Increase image size to 256x1024 to get an 8x32 (256 tokens) feature map. 2) Implement a custom ResizePad transform to preserve aspect ratio. 3) Add 2D Positional Encoding for the image features. 4) Add data augmentation (RandomAffine, ColorJitter). 5) Train for 20 epochs with a warmup + cosine decay learning rate scheduler.

eval_result.eval_log:
[eval_harness] Phase 1: Running predict script: /workspace/predict.py
  checkpoint: /workspace/checkpoints/best.pt
  data_path:  /data
  output:     /experiments/iter_003/eval_result_predictions.json
[eval_harness] Phase 2: Running evaluator
[eval_harness] Result: {'exprate': 0.0292, 'exprate_le1': 0.0734, 'exprate_le2': 0.1351, 'n_test': 1199}

trajectory.description: Observation: The model improved to 0.0717 exprate. The feature map from DenseNet-121 (8x32) is likely too small for complex equations. Analysis: Using ResNet-50 with modified strides (replace_stride_with_dilation) will provide a larger feature map (16x64 = 1024 tokens). Adding a memory_key_padding_mask to the decoder will prevent it from attending to padded regions. Using a smaller learning rate for the pretrained backbone will stabilize training. Decision: Update model to ResNet-50 with dilation, add memory_key_padding_mask, adjust learning rates, and train for 25 epochs.

eval_result.eval_log:
[eval_harness] Phase 1: Running predict script: /workspace/predict.py
  checkpoint: /workspace/checkpoints/best.pt
  data_path:  /data
  output:     /experiments/iter_004/eval_result_predictions.json
[eval_harness] Phase 2: Running evaluator
[eval_harness] Result: {'exprate': 0.1234, 'exprate_le1': 0.221, 'exprate_le2': 0.3103, 'n_test': 1199}

trajectory.description: Observation: The exprate dropped to 0.0292 with ResNet-50. Analysis: ResNet-50 might be harder to train from scratch/fine-tune with the small learning rate used, or the dilation caused issues. DenseNet-121 previously worked better (0.0717) but had a too small feature map (8x32). Decision: Switch back to DenseNet-121, but modify `transition3.pool` to `nn.Identity()` to preserve spatial resolution, resulting in a 16x64 feature map (stride 16 instead of 32). This provides 1024 visual tokens, which is plenty for long equations. I will also use OneCycleLR for faster convergence, add label smoothing, and train for 40 epochs since we have enough time budget.

eval_result.eval_log:
[eval_harness] Phase 1: Running predict script: /workspace/predict.py
  checkpoint: /workspace/checkpoints/best.pt
  data_path:  /data
  output:     /experiments/iter_005/eval_result_predictions.json
[eval_harness] Phase 2: Running evaluator
[eval_harness] Result: {'exprate': 0.1293, 'exprate_le1': 0.2377, 'exprate_le2': 0.3328, 'n_test': 1199}

trajectory.description: Observation: The previous iteration achieved 0.1234 exprate with DenseNet-121 and greedy decoding. Analysis: Beam search is a standard technique to improve sequence generation performance without retraining the model. I will implement beam search with length penalty in predict.py and evaluate the existing best checkpoint to measure the isolated gain of beam search. Decision: Update predict.py with beam search and submit eval immediately.

trajectory.description: Observation: Beam search improved exprate to 0.1293. Analysis: The model capacity can be increased to improve performance. I will increase d_model to 512 and num_decoder_layers to 6. I will also use a smaller learning rate for the pretrained encoder to preserve its features, and increase the batch size to 24 to speed up training. I will train for 80 epochs since we have enough time budget (~2.76 hours left, estimated time ~1.2 hours). Decision: Update model.py, train.py, and predict.py, then train and evaluate.
